title: Harness Migration Challenge Reports — spokes report back; bootstrap learns
what: When a spoke Ozi completes a migration lug, it appends a structured challenge report to the outgoing lug payload and delivers it back to hub. Hub Ozi indexes these reports in harness/migration-reports/. Recurring challenges across multiple spokes are promoted to hygiene rules in deprecated-patterns.json. Each new bootstrap version includes a learning-index.json summarizing aggregated challenge patterns from the previous migration cycle. Spokes consume the learning index before executing migration steps so that known problems are pre-warned.
why: Migration today is unidirectional: hub pushes, spoke receives, and the hub never learns what was hard. Identical problems repeat across spokes and sessions with no collective memory. The challenge report loop closes this feedback gap: the fleet teaches itself what migrations are difficult, and each subsequent bootstrap version is easier than the last.

use_cases.title: Spoke completes migration and reports challenges
use_cases.trigger: Spoke Ozi marks migration lug complete after running audit + file replacement steps
use_cases.outcome: Challenge report appended to lug completion record and delivered to hub via outgoing lug; hub indexes report under harness/migration-reports/{spoke_id}/{from_version}-to-{to_version}.json

use_cases.title: Hub consolidates challenge reports into hygiene rules
use_cases.trigger: Hub Ozi runs consolidation; reads migration-reports/ for all reports in the current version window
use_cases.outcome: Challenges appearing in 3+ reports for the same version transition are promoted to hygiene rules in deprecated-patterns.json; challenge count indexed per version transition in harness-state.json

use_cases.title: New bootstrap version includes learning index
use_cases.outcome: learning-index.json written alongside version-manifest.json; migration lugs delivered to outdated spokes reference the learning index path; spokes read it before executing any migration step

use_cases.title: Recurring challenge escalates to blocker in next version
use_cases.trigger: A challenge pattern from learning-index.json recurs in the next migration cycle (spoke reports same step with severity warn after it was documented)
use_cases.outcome: Hub Ozi escalates severity from warn to must_resolve_before_migration in the following bootstrap version; migration lugs for that version include explicit pre-check for the pattern

patterns.name: Bidirectional migration loop
patterns.description: Migration is not one-direction. Hub pushes migration lug; spoke executes; spoke returns challenge report lug. The return path is as important as the forward path. Hub must acknowledge receipt in its registry before the migration cycle is considered closed.

patterns.name: Aggregation before promotion
patterns.description: A single challenge report does not change hygiene rules. Promotion requires the same challenge step appearing in reports from 3 or more spokes within one version window. This prevents noisy or environment-specific issues from polluting fleet-wide guidance.

patterns.name: Learning index as pre-read contract
patterns.description: Migration lugs must include a step: 'Read learning-index.json before executing migration steps.' The index is not optional documentation — it is a mandatory pre-read that informs the executing agent what problems to watch for before they occur.

patterns.name: Severity escalation for recurring patterns
patterns.description: Documented challenges that recur escalate automatically. warn -> must_resolve_before_migration is a one-way gate: once escalated, the pattern cannot be downgraded without explicit hub Ozi review and a note in the hygiene audit trail.

how.trigger: Spoke Ozi completes a migration lug (any harness version transition)
steps_summary: 1. Spoke Ozi completes migration lug execution — builds challenge_report object with spoke_id, harness_version_from, harness_version_to, challenges[] (each with step, description, severity, resolved_by)
2. Challenge report appended to lug completion record; outgoing lug (type: task, routed_to: HUB) created in spoke/WAI-Spoke/lugs/outgoing/ with payload containing the challenge report
3. Hub Ozi receives outgoing lug on next consolidation; validates report schema; writes to hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/harness/migration-reports/{spoke_id}/{from}-to-{to}.json
4. Hub updates harness-state.json challenge_count for the version transition; challenge index updated

5. At consolidation: Hub reads all migration-reports/ for current version window; aggregates challenges by step — challenges with occurrence_count >= 3 promoted to hygiene rule in deprecated-patterns.json

6. New bootstrap snapshot written; learning-index.json generated from aggregated challenge patterns — one entry per promoted pattern with step_pattern, description, occurrences, severity, pre_warning_text
7. Migration lugs generated for next cycle reference learning-index.json path; migration lug step 1 is always 'Read learning-index.json'

8. On next migration cycle: if a spoke reports a challenge matching an existing learning-index entry, Hub Ozi increments recurrence count; if severity was warn and pattern recurs, escalate to must_resolve_before_migration in following version
constraints: Challenge report is required — migration lugs are not considered complete without a challenge report payload (even if challenges[] is empty)
Hub must confirm receipt of report in registry before migration cycle status is set to closed
Aggregation threshold is 3 reports; configurable in harness-state.json as challenge_promotion_threshold
Severity escalation is permanent within a bootstrap version; downgrade requires hub Ozi review + audit note
learning-index.json must be generated before migration lugs are dispatched for the new version

triggers: Migration lug completion by any spoke Ozi
Consolidation event reading migration-reports/ for the version window
New bootstrap snapshot generation (learning-index.json must be written)
not_when: Spoke that failed migration mid-execution does not submit a challenge report — partial reports are discarded; full report requires migration completion
Hub does not aggregate reports across version windows — each version transition is an independent aggregate
when.frequency: Per migration lug completion (challenge report). Per consolidation event (aggregation + learning index generation).

inputs.name: challenge_report
inputs.description: Structured report produced by spoke Ozi on migration completion; delivered as outgoing lug payload

schema.spoke_id: string — unique spoke identifier from hub-registry.json
schema.harness_version_from: string — semver of the harness version being migrated from
schema.harness_version_to: string — semver of the harness version being migrated to
schema.completed_at: ISO 8601 datetime — when migration lug was marked complete

challenges.step: string — migration step identifier (matches step label in migration lug)
challenges.description: string — what the challenge was, observed behavior, error text if applicable
challenges.severity: enum: warn | block — warn = completed with workaround; block = required manual intervention before proceeding
challenges.resolved_by: string — how it was resolved (free text); null if challenge remains unresolved at report time

inputs.name: harness_migration_reports_index
inputs.description: Hub index of all received challenge reports, keyed by spoke_id and version transition; lives at hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/harness/migration-reports/index.json

reports.report_path: string — relative path to report file

outputs.name: learning_index
outputs.description: Generated alongside each new bootstrap snapshot; consumed by migration lugs as mandatory pre-read; lives at hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/harness/bootstrap/v{N}/learning-index.json

schema.version: string — bootstrap version this learning index accompanies
schema.consolidated_from: string — version transition these patterns were drawn from (e.g. '1.3.0-to-1.4.0')

challenge_patterns.step_pattern: string — migration step label where this challenge was observed
challenge_patterns.description: string — description of the challenge pattern generalized across all reporting spokes
challenge_patterns.occurrences: integer — number of spokes that reported this challenge
challenge_patterns.pre_warning_text: string — text to display to agent before executing this step; should be actionable

outputs.name: hygiene_promotion
outputs.description: Challenges promoted to deprecated-patterns.json when occurrence_count >= challenge_promotion_threshold

state_changes: harness-state.json: challenge_count['{from}-to-{to}'] increments on each report receipt
harness-state.json: challenge_promotion_threshold (default 3) governs when patterns are promoted
deprecated-patterns.json: new hygiene rule added when challenge_count >= threshold
migration-reports/index.json: new entry added on each report receipt
spoke wheel.migration_challenges_submitted = true after report delivered

constraints: Challenge report is mandatory — an empty challenges[] list is valid but the report object itself must always be submitted on migration completion
Hub aggregates reports per version transition only (no cross-window aggregation)
learning-index.json must exist in bootstrap snapshot before migration lugs are dispatched; migration lug generation blocked otherwise
Severity escalation is permanent within a bootstrap version; requires explicit hub Ozi audit note to reverse
Spoke that fails migration mid-run does not submit a challenge report; partial reports are rejected on hub receipt
challenge_promotion_threshold is configurable in harness-state.json but defaults to 3; lowering below 2 is not permitted

tests.description: A migration lug completion record includes a challenge_report object with all required fields (spoke_id, harness_version_from, harness_version_to, completed_at, challenges[]); empty challenges[] is valid
tests.pass_criteria: json.loads(report) succeeds; all required top-level fields present; each challenge entry has step, description, severity, resolved_by

tests.description: After hub Ozi receives a challenge report outgoing lug, migration-reports/index.json contains a new entry for the spoke_id + version_transition pair
tests.pass_criteria: index.json readable; entry present with matching spoke_id and version_transition; received_at set

tests.description: Three challenge reports for the same migration step (same version transition, same step label) result in a new hygiene rule in deprecated-patterns.json
tests.pass_criteria: After processing 3 reports with identical step, deprecated-patterns.json contains entry for that step_pattern; 2 reports do not trigger promotion

tests.description: Hub Ozi cannot dispatch migration lugs for a new bootstrap version if learning-index.json is absent from the snapshot
tests.pass_criteria: Attempt to dispatch migration lugs without learning-index.json raises validation error in hub Ozi; dispatch blocked

tests.description: A warn-level challenge pattern that recurs in the next migration cycle is escalated to must_resolve_before_migration in the subsequent bootstrap version's learning-index.json
tests.pass_criteria: learning-index.json for the following version shows severity: must_resolve_before_migration for the matching step_pattern

health.verification_note: Design spec authored from plan file /home/mario/.claude/plans/how-can-our-design-warm-raven.md Loop 3A and 3B.

perceive: Read this spec (spec-harness-challenge-reports-v1). Read related spec spec-harness-self-maintaining-wheel-v1 for the migration lug contract and consolidation trigger. Check hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/harness/ for current migration-reports/ folder and harness-state.json. Confirm challenge_promotion_threshold field exists in harness-state.json or note that it must be added.
execute: ROUTING: Steps 2 and 3 require modifying tools/ozi_autopilot.py (Basher-owned). Create a Basher impl lug (routed_to: BASHER) for each and deliver to basher/WAI-Spoke/lugs/incoming/ — do NOT edit tools/ directly. Non-Basher steps can be impl lugs here. Full sequence: (1) Add challenge_report field to migration lug schema (modify lug template at templates/migration-lug-template.json — check if this path is Basher-owned first). (2) BASHER LUG: Implement outgoing lug creation in spoke Ozi on migration completion. (3) BASHER LUG: Implement hub receipt + index update in hub Ozi consolidation pass. (4) Implement challenge aggregation logic in a hub-side Python script (not in tools/ — create as a new file in hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/harness/scripts/). (5) Implement learning-index.json generation step in consolidation (same hub-side script). (6) Add mandatory learning-index pre-read step to migration lug template.
verify: All five tests in tests[] pass. learning-index.json present in at least one bootstrap snapshot in the test environment. migration-reports/index.json updated correctly after spoke delivers a challenge report. Aggregation threshold correctly blocks promotion at 2 reports, triggers it at 3.
